question: Which countries were selected as World Cup hosts on December 2, 2010, and for which years?
answer: Russia was selected as the host for the 2018 FIFA World Cup and Qatar for the 2022 FIFA World Cup.
keypoints: FIFA announced Russia as the host of the 2018 FIFA World Cup and Qatar as the host of the 2022 FIFA World Cup on December 2, 2010.

question: Who had exclusive authority to choose FIFA World Cup hosts during the 2010 selection?
answer: The 22 FIFA Executive Committee (ExCo) members held the sole power to select World Cup hosts.
keypoints: The 22 FIFA Executive Committee (ExCo) members held the sole power to select the World Cup hosts.

question: What financial commitment and stadium plan did Russia propose for its 2018 World Cup bid?
answer: Russia pledged to spend 10 billion dollars and use 16 stadiums for its 2018 World Cup bid.
keypoints: Russia pledged to spend 10 billion dollars and use 16 stadiums for its 2018 World Cup bid.

question: How much did Qatar pledge to spend on infrastructure and how many stadiums for its 2022 World Cup bid?
answer: Qatar pledged to spend 200 billion dollars on infrastructure and build 12 new air-conditioned stadiums.
keypoints: Qatar pledged to spend 200 billion dollars on infrastructure and build 12 new air-conditioned stadiums for its 2022 World Cup bid.

question: What did FIFA Executive Committee member Rafael Salguero admit regarding Russia's 2018 bid?
answer: Rafael Salguero admitted agreeing to accept $1 million for his vote from Russia.
keypoints: ExCo member Rafael Salguero admitted agreeing to accept $1 million for his vote from Russia.

question: How much money did Jack Warner allegedly receive from Russia in exchange for his World Cup vote?
answer: Jack Warner allegedly received $5 million for his vote from Russia.
keypoints: Jack Warner allegedly received $5 million for his vote from Russia.

question: What did British journalists allege about payments to Jack Warner from Qatar?
answer: British journalists alleged a Qatari-owned company paid Jack Warner around $2 million for his vote.
keypoints: British journalists alleged a Qatari-owned company paid Jack Warner around $2 million for his vote.

question: How many additional FIFA Executive Committee members faced bribery allegations related to Qatar's bid?
answer: Allegations emerged that Qatar made payments to three other ExCo members.
keypoints: Allegations emerged that Qatar made payments to three other ExCo members.

question: How did Germany allegedly secure its 2006 World Cup hosting rights?
answer: Germany won the 2006 World Cup bid after allegedly bribing at least four ExCo members.
keypoints: Germany won the 2006 World Cup bid after allegedly bribing at least four ExCo members.

question: What controversy surrounded South Africa's 2010 World Cup hosting selection?
answer: South Africa won the 2010 World Cup bid amid later allegations it paid ExCo members for votes.
keypoints: South Africa won the 2010 World Cup bid amid later allegations it paid ExCo members for votes.

question: When did FIFA transfer World Cup host voting authority to its Executive Committee?
answer: FIFA shifted the World Cup host vote from its full Congress to the 24-member Executive Committee (ExCo) in 1964.
keypoints: FIFA shifted the World Cup host vote from its full Congress to the 24-member Executive Committee (ExCo) in 1964.

question: How many Executive Committee votes were required to win a FIFA World Cup hosting bid?
answer: FIFA required only 13 votes from the ExCo to win a World Cup hosting bid.
keypoints: FIFA required only 13 votes from the ExCo to win a World Cup hosting bid.

question: What action did the FBI take against FIFA officials in Zurich in 2015?
answer: The FBI arrested several FIFA officials in Zurich in 2015 and launched investigations into decades of FIFA dealings.
keypoints: The FBI arrested several FIFA officials in Zurich in 2015 and launched investigations into decades of FIFA dealings.

question: How many of the 22 Executive Committee members from the 2010 host vote faced sanctions?
answer: 13 of the 22 ExCo members present at the 2010 host selection vote were later indicted or banned from FIFA.
keypoints: 13 of the 22 ExCo members present at the 2010 host selection vote were later indicted or banned from FIFA.

question: Why did FIFA President Sepp Blatter resign?
answer: Sepp Blatter resigned in disgrace following the FIFA corruption scandals.
keypoints: FIFA President Sepp Blatter resigned in disgrace following the corruption scandals.

question: When did FIFA restore World Cup host voting to its full Congress?
answer: FIFA returned the World Cup host vote to its full Congress in 2016.
keypoints: FIFA returned the World Cup host vote to its full Congress in 2016.
